v.CNV: 那时，全世界只有一种语言，大家说同样的话语。
v.ESV: Now the whole earth had one language and the same words.
v.GAE: 온 땅의 언어가 하나요 말이 하나였더라
v.KJV: And the whole earth was of one language, and of one speech.
v.KRV: 온 땅의 구음이 하나이요 언어가 하나이었더라
v.NASB: Now the whole earth used the same language and the same words.
v.NIV: Now the whole world had one language and a common speech.
v.NRSV: Now the whole earth had one language and the same words.
v.SAENEW: 처음에 세상에는 언어가 하나뿐이어서, 모두가 같은 말을 썼다.
v.WLB: 당시 온 세상에는 언어가 하나였으며 같은 말을 썼습니다.

v.CNV: 他们向东迁移的时候，在示拿地发现一块平原，就住在那里。
v.ESV: And as people migrated from the east, they found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.
v.GAE: 이에 그들이 동방으로 옮기다가 시날 평지를 만나 거기 거류하며
v.KJV: And it came to pass, as they journeyed from the east, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar; and they dwelt there.
v.KRV: 이에 그들이 동방으로 옮기다가 시날 평지를 만나 거기 거하고
v.NASB: It came about as they journeyed east, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.
v.NIV: As people moved eastward, they found a plain in Shinar and settled there.
v.NRSV: And as they migrated from the east, they came upon a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.
v.SAENEW: 사람들이 동쪽에서 이동하여 오다가, 시날 땅 한 들판에 이르러서, 거기에 자리를 잡았다.
v.WLB: 사람들이 동쪽에서 와서 시날 땅에서 평원을 발견하고는 그곳에 정착했습니다.

v.CNV: 他们彼此说：“来，我们做砖，把砖烧透吧！”他们就把砖当作石头，又把石漆当作灰泥。
v.ESV: And they said to one another, “Come, let us make bricks, and burn them thoroughly.” And they had brick for stone, and bitumen for mortar.
v.GAE: 서로 말하되 자, 벽돌을 만들어 견고히 굽자 하고 이에 벽돌로 돌을 대신하며 역청으로 진흙을 대신하고
v.KJV: And they said one to another, Go to, let us make brick, and burn them throughly. And they had brick for stone, and slime had they for morter.
v.KRV: 서로 말하되 자, 벽돌을 만들어 견고히 굽자 하고 이에 벽돌로 돌을 대신하며 역청으로 진흙을 대신하고
v.NASB: They said to one another, 'Come, let us make bricks and burn them thoroughly.' And they used brick for stone, and they used tar for mortar.
v.NIV: They said to each other, “Come, let’s make bricks and bake them thoroughly.” They used brick instead of stone, and tar for mortar.
v.NRSV: And they said to one another, “Come, let us make bricks, and burn them thoroughly.” And they had brick for stone, and bitumen for mortar.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 서로 말하였다. "자, 벽돌을 빚어서, 단단히 구워내자." 사람들은 돌 대신에 벽돌을 쓰고, 흙 대신에 역청을 썼다.
v.WLB: 그들이 서로 말했습니다. “자, 우리가 벽돌을 만들어 단단하게 굽자.” 그들은 돌 대신 벽돌을, 진흙 대신 역청을 사용했습니다.

v.CNV: 他们又说：“来，我们建一座城，造一座塔，塔顶要通天。我们要为自己立名，免得分散在全地上。”
v.ESV: Then they said, “Come, let us build ourselves a city and a tower with its top in the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves, lest we be dispersed over the face of the whole earth.”
v.GAE: 또 말하되 자, 성읍과 탑을 건설하여 그 탑 꼭대기를 하늘에 닿게 하여 우리 이름을 내고 온 지면에 흩어짐을 면하자 하였더니
v.KJV: And they said, Go to, let us build us a city and a tower, whose top may reach unto heaven; and let us make us a name, lest we be scattered abroad upon the face of the whole earth.
v.KRV: 또 말하되 자, 성과 대를 쌓아 대 꼭대기를 하늘에 닿게하여 우리 이름을 내고 온 지면에 흩어짐을 면하자 하였더니
v.NASB: They said, 'Come, let us build for ourselves a city, and a tower whose top will reach into heaven, and let us make for ourselves a name, otherwise we will be scattered abroad over the face of the whole earth.'
v.NIV: Then they said, “Come, let us build ourselves a city, with a tower that reaches to the heavens, so that we may make a name for ourselves; otherwise we will be scattered over the face of the whole earth.”
v.NRSV: Then they said, “Come, let us build ourselves a city, and a tower with its top in the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves; otherwise we shall be scattered abroad upon the face of the whole earth.”
v.SAENEW: 그들은 또 말하였다. "자, 도시를 세우고, 그 안에 탑을 쌓고서, 탑 꼭대기가 하늘에 닿게 하여, 우리의 이름을 날리고, 온 땅 위에 흩어지지 않게 하자."
v.WLB: 그리고 그들이 말했습니다. “자, 우리가 우리를 위해 성을 쌓고 하늘까지 닿는 탑을 쌓자. 우리를 위해 이름을 내고 온 지면에 흩어지지 않게 하자.”

v.CNV: 耶和华下来，要看看世人建造的城和塔。
v.ESV: And the Lord came down to see the city and the tower, which the children of man had built.
v.GAE: 여호와께서 사람들이 건설하는 그 성읍과 탑을 보려고 내려오셨더라
v.KJV: And the LORD came down to see the city and the tower, which the children of men builded.
v.KRV: 여호와께서 인생들의 쌓는 성과 대를 보시려고 강림하셨더라
v.NASB: The LORD came down to see the city and the tower which the sons of men had built.
v.NIV: But the Lord came down to see the city and the tower the people were building.
v.NRSV: The LORD came down to see the city and the tower, which mortals had built.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 사람들이 짓고 있는 도시와 탑을 보려고 내려오셨다.
v.WLB: 여호와께서 사람들이 쌓는 성과 탑을 보시기 위해 내려오셨습니다.

v.CNV: 耶和华说：“看哪，他们同是一个民族，有一样的语言，他们一开始就作这事，以后他们所要作的一切，就没有可以拦阻他们的了。
v.ESV: And the Lord said, “Behold, they are one people, and they have all one language, and this is only the beginning of what they will do. And nothing that they propose to do will now be impossible for them.
v.GAE: 여호와께서 이르시되 이 무리가 한 족속이요 언어도 하나이므로 이같이 시작하였으니 이 후로는 그 하고자 하는 일을 막을 수 없으리로다
v.KJV: And the LORD said, Behold, the people is one, and they have all one language; and this they begin to do: and now nothing will be restrained from them, which they have imagined to do.
v.KRV: 여호와께서 가라사대 이 무리가 한 족속이요 언어도 하나이므로 이같이 시작하였으니 이후로는 그 경영하는 일을 금지할 수 없으리로다
v.NASB: The LORD said, 'Behold, they are one people, and they all have the same language. And this is what they began to do, and now nothing which they purpose to do will be impossible for them.
v.NIV: The Lord said, “If as one people speaking the same language they have begun to do this, then nothing they plan to do will be impossible for them.
v.NRSV: And the LORD said, “Look, they are one people, and they have all one language; and this is only the beginning of what they will do; nothing that they propose to do will now be impossible for them.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 말씀하셨다. "보아라, 만일 사람들이 같은 말을 쓰는 한 백성으로서, 이렇게 이런 일을 하기 시작하였으니, 이제 그들은, 하고자 하는 것은 무엇이든지, 하지 못할 일이 없을 것이다.
v.WLB: 그리고 여호와께서 말씀하셨습니다. “저들이 한 민족으로서 모두 한 언어로 말하고 있어 이런 짓을 벌이기 시작했다. 그러니 이제 그들이 하고자 꾸미는 일이라면 못할 게 없을 것이다.

v.CNV: 来，我们下去，在那里混乱他们的语言，使他们听不懂对方的话。”
v.ESV: Come, let us go down and there confuse their language, so that they may not understand one another's speech.”
v.GAE: 자, 우리가 내려가서 거기서 그들의 언어를 혼잡하게 하여 그들이 서로 알아듣지 못하게 하자 하시고
v.KJV: Go to, let us go down, and there confound their language, that they may not understand one another’s speech.
v.KRV: 자, 우리가 내려가서 거기서 그들의 언어를 혼잡케 하여 그들로 서로 알아듣지 못하게 하자 하시고
v.NASB: 'Come, let Us go down and there confuse their language, so that they will not understand one another's speech.'
v.NIV: Come, let us go down and confuse their language so they will not understand each other.”
v.NRSV: Come, let us go down, and confuse their language there, so that they will not understand one another’s speech.”
v.SAENEW: 자, 우리가 내려가서, 그들이 거기에서 하는 말을 뒤섞어서, 그들이 서로 알아듣지 못하게 하자."
v.WLB: 자, 우리가 내려가서 거기에서 그들의 언어를 혼란하게 해 서로 알아듣지 못하게 하자.”

v.CNV: 于是，耶和华把他们从那里分散到全地上，他们就停止建造那城。
v.ESV: So the Lord dispersed them from there over the face of all the earth, and they left off building the city.
v.GAE: 여호와께서 거기서 그들을 온 지면에 흩으셨으므로 그들이 그 도시를 건설하기를 그쳤더라
v.KJV: So the LORD scattered them abroad from thence upon the face of all the earth: and they left off to build the city.
v.KRV: 여호와께서 거기서 그들을 온 지면에 흩으신고로 그들이 성 쌓기를 그쳤더라
v.NASB: So the LORD scattered them abroad from there over the face of the whole earth; and they stopped building the city.
v.NIV: So the Lord scattered them from there over all the earth, and they stopped building the city.
v.NRSV: So the LORD scattered them abroad from there over the face of all the earth, and they left off building the city.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 거기에서 그들을 온 땅으로 흩으셨다. 그래서 그들은 도시 세우는 일을 그만두었다.
v.WLB: 그리하여 여호와께서는 그들을 그곳에서부터 온 땅에 흩어 버리셨습니다. 그들은 성 쌓는 것을 그쳤습니다.

v.CNV: 因此，那城的名就叫巴别，因为耶和华在那里混乱了全地所有的人的语言，又从那里把他们分散在全地上。
v.ESV: Therefore its name was called Babel, because there the Lord confused the language of all the earth. And from there the Lord dispersed them over the face of all the earth.
v.GAE: 그러므로 그 이름을 바벨이라 하니 이는 여호와께서 거기서 온 땅의 언어를 혼잡하게 하셨음이니라 여호와께서 거기서 그들을 온 지면에 흩으셨더라
v.KJV: Therefore is the name of it called Babel; because the LORD did there confound the language of all the earth: and from thence did the LORD scatter them abroad upon the face of all the earth.
v.KRV: 그러므로 그 이름을 바벨이라 하니 이는 여호와께서 거기서 온 땅의 언어를 혼잡케 하셨음이라 여호와께서 거기서 그들을 온 지면에 흩으셨더라
v.NASB: Therefore its name was called Babel, because there the LORD confused the language of the whole earth; and from there the LORD scattered them abroad over the face of the whole earth.
v.NIV: That is why it was called Babel —because there the Lord confused the language of the whole world. From there the Lord scattered them over the face of the whole earth.
v.NRSV: Therefore it was called Babel, because there the LORD confused the language of all the earth; and from there the LORD scattered them abroad over the face of all the earth.
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 거기에서 온 세상의 말을 뒤섞으셨다고 하여, 사람들은 그 곳의 이름을 바벨이라고 한다. 주님께서 거기에서 사람들을 온 땅에 흩으셨다.
v.WLB: 그래서 그곳 이름이 바벨이라 불리는 것입니다. 그곳에서 여호와께서 온 세상의 언어를 혼란하게 하셨기 때문입니다. 그곳에서 여호와께서 그들을 온 땅에 흩으셨습니다.

v.CNV: 以下是闪的后代。洪水以后两年，闪一百岁的时候，生了亚法撒。
v.ESV: These are the generations of Shem. When Shem was 100 years old, he fathered Arpachshad two years after the flood.
v.GAE: 셈의 족보는 이러하니라 셈은 백 세 곧 홍수 후 이 년에 아르박삿을 낳았고
v.KJV: These are the generations of Shem: Shem was an hundred years old, and begat Arphaxad two years after the flood:
v.KRV: 셈의 후예는 이러하니라 셈은 일백세 곧 홍수 후 이년에 아르박삿을 낳았고
v.NASB: These are the records of the generations of Shem. Shem was one hundred years old, and became the father of Arpachshad two years after the flood;
v.NIV: This is the account of Shem’s family line. Two years after the flood, when Shem was 100 years old, he became the father of Arphaxad.
v.NRSV: These are the descendants of Shem. When Shem was one hundred years old, he became the father of Arpachshad two years after the flood;
v.SAENEW: 셈의 족보는 이러하다. 셈은, 홍수가 끝난 지 이 년 뒤, 백 살이 되었을 때에 아르박삿을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 셈의 족보는 이러합니다. 셈은 홍수 후 2년 뒤인 100세에 아르박삿을 낳았습니다.

v.CNV: 闪生亚法撒以后，又活了五百年，并且生了其他的儿女。
v.ESV: And Shem lived after he fathered Arpachshad 500 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.GAE: 아르박삿을 낳은 후에 오백 년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.KJV: And Shem lived after he begat Arphaxad five hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.
v.KRV: 아르박삿을 낳은 후에 오백년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.NASB: and Shem lived five hundred years after he became the father of Arpachshad, and he had other sons and daughters.
v.NIV: And after he became the father of Arphaxad, Shem lived 500 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.NRSV: and Shem lived after the birth of Arpachshad five hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
v.SAENEW: 셈은 아르박삿을 낳은 뒤에, 오백 년을 더 살면서 아들딸을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 셈은 아르박삿을 낳은 후 500년을 더 살면서 다른 자녀들을 낳았습니다.

v.CNV: 亚法撒活到三十五岁，就生了沙拉。
v.ESV: When Arpachshad had lived 35 years, he fathered Shelah.
v.GAE: 아르박삿은 삼십오 세에 셀라를 낳았고
v.KJV: And Arphaxad lived five and thirty years, and begat Salah:
v.KRV: 아르박삿은 삼십 오세에 셀라를 낳았고
v.NASB: Arpachshad lived thirty-five years, and became the father of Shelah;
v.NIV: When Arphaxad had lived 35 years, he became the father of Shelah.
v.NRSV: When Arpachshad had lived thirty-five years, he became the father of Shelah;
v.SAENEW: 아르박삿은 서른다섯 살에 셀라를 낳았다.
v.WLB: 아르박삿은 35세에 셀라를 낳았고

v.CNV: 亚法撒生沙拉以后，又活了四百零三年，并且生了其他的儿女。
v.ESV: And Arpachshad lived after he fathered Shelah 403 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.GAE: 셀라를 낳은 후에 사백삼 년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.KJV: And Arphaxad lived after he begat Salah four hundred and three years, and begat sons and daughters.
v.KRV: 셀라를 낳은 후에 사백 삼년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.NASB: and Arpachshad lived four hundred and three years after he became the father of Shelah, and he had other sons and daughters.
v.NIV: And after he became the father of Shelah, Arphaxad lived 403 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.NRSV: and Arpachshad lived after the birth of Shelah four hundred three years, and had other sons and daughters.
v.SAENEW: 아르박삿은 셀라를 낳은 뒤에, 사백삼 년을 더 살면서 아들딸을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 셀라를 낳은 후 403년을 더 살면서 다른 자녀들을 낳았습니다.

v.CNV: 沙拉活到三十岁，就生了希伯。
v.ESV: When Shelah had lived 30 years, he fathered Eber.
v.GAE: 셀라는 삼십 세에 에벨을 낳았고
v.KJV: And Salah lived thirty years, and begat Eber:
v.KRV: 셀라는 삼십세에 에벨을 낳았고
v.NASB: Shelah lived thirty years, and became the father of Eber;
v.NIV: When Shelah had lived 30 years, he became the father of Eber.
v.NRSV: When Shelah had lived thirty years, he became the father of Eber;
v.SAENEW: 셀라는 서른 살에 에벨을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 셀라는 30세에 에벨을 낳았고

v.CNV: 沙拉生希伯以后，又活了四百零三年，并且生了其他的儿女。
v.ESV: And Shelah lived after he fathered Eber 403 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.GAE: 에벨을 낳은 후에 사백삼 년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.KJV: And Salah lived after he begat Eber four hundred and three years, and begat sons and daughters.
v.KRV: 에벨을 낳은 후에 사백 삼년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.NASB: and Shelah lived four hundred and three years after he became the father of Eber, and he had other sons and daughters.
v.NIV: And after he became the father of Eber, Shelah lived 403 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.NRSV: and Shelah lived after the birth of Eber four hundred three years, and had other sons and daughters.
v.SAENEW: 셀라는 에벨을 낳은 뒤에, 사백삼 년을 더 살면서 아들딸을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 에벨을 낳은 후 403년을 더 살면서 다른 자녀들을 낳았습니다.

v.CNV: 希伯活到三十四岁，就生了法勒。
v.ESV: When Eber had lived 34 years, he fathered Peleg.
v.GAE: 에벨은 삼십사 세에 벨렉을 낳았고
v.KJV: And Eber lived four and thirty years, and begat Peleg:
v.KRV: 에벨은 삼십 사세에 벨렉을 낳았고
v.NASB: Eber lived thirty-four years, and became the father of Peleg;
v.NIV: When Eber had lived 34 years, he became the father of Peleg.
v.NRSV: When Eber had lived thirty-four years, he became the father of Peleg;
v.SAENEW: 에벨은 서른네 살에 벨렉을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 에벨은 34세에 벨렉을 낳았고

v.CNV: 希伯生法勒以后，又活了四百三十年，并且生了其他的儿女。
v.ESV: And Eber lived after he fathered Peleg 430 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.GAE: 벨렉을 낳은 후에 사백삼십 년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.KJV: And Eber lived after he begat Peleg four hundred and thirty years, and begat sons and daughters.
v.KRV: 벨렉을 낳은 후에 사백 삼십년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.NASB: and Eber lived four hundred and thirty years after he became the father of Peleg, and he had other sons and daughters.
v.NIV: And after he became the father of Peleg, Eber lived 430 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.NRSV: and Eber lived after the birth of Peleg four hundred thirty years, and had other sons and daughters.
v.SAENEW: 에벨은 벨렉을 낳은 뒤에, 사백삼십 년을 더 살면서 아들딸을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 벨렉을 낳은 후 430년을 더 살면서 다른 자녀들을 낳았습니다.

v.CNV: 法勒活到三十岁，就生了拉吴。
v.ESV: When Peleg had lived 30 years, he fathered Reu.
v.GAE: 벨렉은 삼십 세에 르우를 낳았고
v.KJV: And Peleg lived thirty years, and begat Reu:
v.KRV: 벨렉은 삼십세에 르우를 낳았고
v.NASB: Peleg lived thirty years, and became the father of Reu;
v.NIV: When Peleg had lived 30 years, he became the father of Reu.
v.NRSV: When Peleg had lived thirty years, he became the father of Reu;
v.SAENEW: 벨렉은 서른 살에 르우를 낳았다.
v.WLB: 벨렉은 30세에 르우를 낳았고

v.CNV: 法勒生拉吴以后，又活了二百零九年，并且生了其他的儿女。
v.ESV: And Peleg lived after he fathered Reu 209 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.GAE: 르우를 낳은 후에 이백구 년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.KJV: And Peleg lived after he begat Reu two hundred and nine years, and begat sons and daughters.
v.KRV: 르우를 낳은 후에 이백 구년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.NASB: and Peleg lived two hundred and nine years after he became the father of Reu, and he had other sons and daughters.
v.NIV: And after he became the father of Reu, Peleg lived 209 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.NRSV: and Peleg lived after the birth of Reu two hundred nine years, and had other sons and daughters.
v.SAENEW: 벨렉은 르우를 낳은 뒤에, 이백구 년을 더 살면서 아들딸을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 르우를 낳은 후 209년을 더 살면서 다른 자녀들을 낳았습니다.

v.CNV: 拉吴活到三十二岁，就生了西鹿。
v.ESV: When Reu had lived 32 years, he fathered Serug.
v.GAE: 르우는 삼십이 세에 스룩을 낳았고
v.KJV: And Reu lived two and thirty years, and begat Serug:
v.KRV: 르우는 삼십 이세에 스룩을 낳았고
v.NASB: Reu lived thirty-two years, and became the father of Serug;
v.NIV: When Reu had lived 32 years, he became the father of Serug.
v.NRSV: When Reu had lived thirty-two years, he became the father of Serug;
v.SAENEW: 르우는 서른두 살에 스룩을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 르우는 32세에 스룩을 낳았고

v.CNV: 拉吴生西鹿以后，又活了二百零七年，并且生了其他的儿女。
v.ESV: And Reu lived after he fathered Serug 207 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.GAE: 스룩을 낳은 후에 이백칠 년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.KJV: And Reu lived after he begat Serug two hundred and seven years, and begat sons and daughters.
v.KRV: 스룩을 낳은 후에 이백 칠년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.NASB: and Reu lived two hundred and seven years after he became the father of Serug, and he had other sons and daughters.
v.NIV: And after he became the father of Serug, Reu lived 207 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.NRSV: and Reu lived after the birth of Serug two hundred seven years, and had other sons and daughters.
v.SAENEW: 르우는 스룩을 낳은 뒤에, 이백칠 년을 더 살면서 아들딸을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 스룩을 낳은 후 207년을 더 살면서 다른 자녀들을 낳았습니다.

v.CNV: 西鹿活到三十岁，就生了拿鹤。
v.ESV: When Serug had lived 30 years, he fathered Nahor.
v.GAE: 스룩은 삼십 세에 나홀을 낳았고
v.KJV: And Serug lived thirty years, and begat Nahor:
v.KRV: 스룩은 삼십세에 나홀을 낳았고
v.NASB: Serug lived thirty years, and became the father of Nahor;
v.NIV: When Serug had lived 30 years, he became the father of Nahor.
v.NRSV: When Serug had lived thirty years, he became the father of Nahor;
v.SAENEW: 스룩은 서른 살에 나홀을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 스룩은 30세에 나홀을 낳았고

v.CNV: 西鹿生拿鹤以后，又活了二百年，并且生了其他的儿女。
v.ESV: And Serug lived after he fathered Nahor 200 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.GAE: 나홀을 낳은 후에 이백 년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.KJV: And Serug lived after he begat Nahor two hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.
v.KRV: 나홀을 낳은 후에 이백년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.NASB: and Serug lived two hundred years after he became the father of Nahor, and he had other sons and daughters.
v.NIV: And after he became the father of Nahor, Serug lived 200 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.NRSV: and Serug lived after the birth of Nahor two hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
v.SAENEW: 스룩은 나홀을 낳은 뒤에, 이백 년을 더 살면서 아들딸을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 나홀을 낳은 후 200년을 더 살면서 다른 자녀들을 낳았습니다.

v.CNV: 拿鹤活到二十九岁，就生了他拉。
v.ESV: When Nahor had lived 29 years, he fathered Terah.
v.GAE: 나홀은 이십구 세에 데라를 낳았고
v.KJV: And Nahor lived nine and twenty years, and begat Terah:
v.KRV: 나홀은 이십 구세에 데라를 낳았고
v.NASB: Nahor lived twenty-nine years, and became the father of Terah;
v.NIV: When Nahor had lived 29 years, he became the father of Terah.
v.NRSV: When Nahor had lived twenty-nine years, he became the father of Terah;
v.SAENEW: 나홀은 스물아홉 살에 데라를 낳았다.
v.WLB: 나홀은 29세에 데라를 낳았고

v.CNV: 拿鹤生他拉以后，又活了一百一十九年，并且生了其他的儿女。
v.ESV: And Nahor lived after he fathered Terah 119 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.GAE: 데라를 낳은 후에 백십구 년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.KJV: And Nahor lived after he begat Terah an hundred and nineteen years, and begat sons and daughters.
v.KRV: 데라를 낳은 후에 일백 십구년을 지내며 자녀를 낳았으며
v.NASB: and Nahor lived one hundred and nineteen years after he became the father of Terah, and he had other sons and daughters.
v.NIV: And after he became the father of Terah, Nahor lived 119 years and had other sons and daughters.
v.NRSV: and Nahor lived after the birth of Terah one hundred nineteen years, and had other sons and daughters.
v.SAENEW: 나홀은 데라를 낳은 뒤에, 백십구 년을 더 살면서 아들딸을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 데라를 낳은 후 119년을 더 살면서 다른 자녀들을 낳았습니다.

v.CNV: 他拉活到七十岁，就生了亚伯兰、拿鹤和哈兰。
v.ESV: When Terah had lived 70 years, he fathered Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
v.GAE: 데라는 칠십 세에 아브람과 나홀과 하란을 낳았더라
v.KJV: And Terah lived seventy years, and begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
v.KRV: 데라는 칠십세에 아브람과 나홀과 하란을 낳았더라
v.NASB: Terah lived seventy years, and became the father of Abram, Nahor and Haran.
v.NIV: After Terah had lived 70 years, he became the father of Abram, Nahor and Haran.
v.NRSV: When Terah had lived seventy years, he became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
v.SAENEW: 데라는 일흔 살에 아브람과 나홀과 하란을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 데라는 70세에 아브람, 나홀, 하란을 낳았습니다.

v.CNV: 以下是他拉的后代：他拉生亚伯兰、拿鹤和哈兰；哈兰生罗得。
v.ESV: Now these are the generations of Terah. Terah fathered Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran fathered Lot.
v.GAE: 데라의 족보는 이러하니라 데라는 아브람과 나홀과 하란을 낳고 하란은 롯을 낳았으며
v.KJV: Now these are the generations of Terah: Terah begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran begat Lot.
v.KRV: 데라의 후예는 이러하니라 데라는 아브람과 나홀과 하란을 낳았고 하란은 롯을 낳았으며
v.NASB: Now these are the records of the generations of Terah. Terah became the father of Abram, Nahor and Haran; and Haran became the father of Lot.
v.NIV: This is the account of Terah’s family line. Terah became the father of Abram, Nahor and Haran. And Haran became the father of Lot.
v.NRSV: Now these are the descendants of Terah. Terah was the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran was the father of Lot.
v.SAENEW: 데라의 족보는 이러하다. 데라는 아브람과 나홀과 하란을 낳았다. 하란은 롯을 낳았다.
v.WLB: 데라의 족보는 이러합니다. 데라는 아브람, 나홀, 하란을 낳았습니다. 하란은 롯을 낳았습니다.

v.CNV: 哈兰在他父亲他拉之前，死在他出生之地，就是迦勒底的吾珥。
v.ESV: Haran died in the presence of his father Terah in the land of his kindred, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
v.GAE: 하란은 그 아비 데라보다 먼저 고향 갈대아인의 우르에서 죽었더라
v.KJV: And Haran died before his father Terah in the land of his nativity, in Ur of the Chaldees.
v.KRV: 하란은 그 아비 데라보다 먼저 본토 갈대아 우르에서 죽었더라
v.NASB: Haran died in the presence of his father Terah in the land of his birth, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
v.NIV: While his father Terah was still alive, Haran died in Ur of the Chaldeans, in the land of his birth.
v.NRSV: Haran died before his father Terah in the land of his birth, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 하란은 그가 태어난 땅 바빌로니아의 우르에서 아버지보다 먼저 죽었다.
v.WLB: 하란은 자기가 태어난 갈대아의 우르에서 자기 아버지 데라보다 먼저 죽었습니다.

v.CNV: 亚伯兰和拿鹤都娶了妻子；亚伯兰的妻子名叫撒莱；拿鹤的妻子名叫密迦，是哈兰的女儿；哈兰是密迦和亦迦的父亲。
v.ESV: And Abram and Nahor took wives. The name of Abram's wife was Sarai, and the name of Nahor's wife, Milcah, the daughter of Haran the father of Milcah and Iscah.
v.GAE: 아브람과 나홀이 장가 들었으니 아브람의 아내의 이름은 사래며 나홀의 아내의 이름은 밀가니 하란의 딸이요 하란은 밀가의 아버지이며 또 이스가의 아버지더라
v.KJV: And Abram and Nahor took them wives: the name of Abram’s wife was Sarai; and the name of Nahor’s wife, Milcah, the daughter of Haran, the father of Milcah, and the father of Iscah.
v.KRV: 아브람과 나홀이 장가 들었으니 아브람의 아내 이름은 사래며 나홀의 아내 이름은 밀가니 하란의 딸이요 하란은 밀가의 아비며 또 이스가의 아비더라
v.NASB: Abram and Nahor took wives for themselves. The name of Abram's wife was Sarai; and the name of Nahor's wife was Milcah, the daughter of Haran, the father of Milcah and Iscah.
v.NIV: Abram and Nahor both married. The name of Abram’s wife was Sarai, and the name of Nahor’s wife was Milkah; she was the daughter of Haran, the father of both Milkah and Iskah.
v.NRSV: Abram and Nahor took wives; the name of Abram’s wife was Sarai, and the name of Nahor’s wife was Milcah. She was the daughter of Haran the father of Milcah and Iscah.
v.SAENEW: 아브람과 나홀이 아내를 맞아들였다. 아브람의 아내의 이름은 사래이고, 나홀의 아내의 이름은 밀가이다. 하란은 밀가와 이스가의 아버지이다.
v.WLB: 아브람과 나홀은 장가를 갔습니다. 아브람의 아내의 이름은 사래였고 나홀의 아내의 이름은 밀가였습니다. 밀가는 하란의 딸이었으며 하란은 밀가와 이스가의 아버지입니다.

v.CNV: 撒莱不能生育，没有孩子。
v.ESV: Now Sarai was barren; she had no child.
v.GAE: 사래는 임신하지 못하므로 자식이 없었더라
v.KJV: But Sarai was barren; she had no child.
v.KRV: 사래는 잉태하지 못하므로 자식이 없었더라
v.NASB: Sarai was barren; she had no child.
v.NIV: Now Sarai was childless because she was not able to conceive.
v.NRSV: Now Sarai was barren; she had no child.
v.SAENEW: 사래는 임신을 하지 못하여서, 자식이 없었다.
v.WLB: 사래는 아기를 낳지 못해서 자식이 없었습니다.

v.CNV: 他拉带着他的儿子亚伯兰和他的孙子，就是哈兰的儿子罗得，以及他的媳妇亚伯兰的妻子撒莱，一同出了迦勒底的吾珥，要到迦南地去；他们到了哈兰，就住在那里。
v.ESV: Terah took Abram his son and Lot the son of Haran, his grandson, and Sarai his daughter-in-law, his son Abram's wife, and they went forth together from Ur of the Chaldeans to go into the land of Canaan, but when they came to Haran, they settled there.
v.GAE: 데라가 그 아들 아브람과 하란의 아들인 그의 손자 롯과 그의 며느리 아브람의 아내 사래를 데리고 갈대아인의 우르를 떠나 가나안 땅으로 가고자 하더니 하란에 이르러 거기 거류하였으며
v.KJV: And Terah took Abram his son, and Lot the son of Haran his son’s son, and Sarai his daughter in law, his son Abram’s wife; and they went forth with them from Ur of the Chaldees, to go into the land of Canaan; and they came unto Haran, and dwelt there.
v.KRV: 데라가 그 아들 아브람과 하란의 아들 그 손자 롯과 그 자부 아브람의 아내 사래를 데리고 갈대아 우르에서 떠나 가나안 땅으로 가고자 하더니 하란에 이르러 거기 거하였으며
v.NASB: Terah took Abram his son, and Lot the son of Haran, his grandson, and Sarai his daughter-in-law, his son Abram's wife; and they went out together from Ur of the Chaldeans in order to enter the land of Canaan; and they went as far as Haran, and settled there.
v.NIV: Terah took his son Abram, his grandson Lot son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai, the wife of his son Abram, and together they set out from Ur of the Chaldeans to go to Canaan. But when they came to Harran, they settled there.
v.NRSV: Terah took his son Abram and his grandson Lot son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai, his son Abram’s wife, and they went out together from Ur of the Chaldeans to go into the land of Canaan; but when they came to Haran, they settled there.
v.SAENEW: 데라는, 아들 아브람과, 하란에게서 난 손자 롯과, 아들 아브람의 아내인 며느리 사래를 데리고, 가나안 땅으로 오려고 바빌로니아의 우르를 떠나서, 하란에 이르렀다. 그는 거기에다가 자리를 잡고 살았다.
v.WLB: 데라는 자기 아들인 아브람 그리고 자기 아들 하란의 아들인 손자 롯 그리고 자기 아들 아브람의 아내이자 자기 며느리인 사래를 데리고 함께 갈대아의 우르를 떠나 가나안으로 향했습니다. 그러나 그들은 하란에 이르러 거기에서 살게 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 他拉死在哈兰；他的寿数共二百零五岁。
v.ESV: The days of Terah were 205 years, and Terah died in Haran.
v.GAE: 데라는 나이가 이백오 세가 되어 하란에서 죽었더라
v.KJV: And the days of Terah were two hundred and five years: and Terah died in Haran.
v.KRV: 데라는 이백 오세를 향수하고 하란에서 죽었더라
v.NASB: The days of Terah were two hundred and five years; and Terah died in Haran.
v.NIV: Terah lived 205 years, and he died in Harran.
v.NRSV: The days of Terah were two hundred five years; and Terah died in Haran.
v.SAENEW: 데라는 이백오 년을 살다가 하란에서 죽었다.
v.WLB: 데라는 205세까지 살다가 하란에서 죽었습니다.
